task: work on the mobile design and functionality including mobile menu, navbar etc for the main page and profiles user and admin. Also work on the user profile in general how saved should be displayed, and listed and all etc. All these production ready and beautiful

stages.spec:
STAGE 1 — Main site mobile navbar + mobile menu polish.

SCOPE (only these files):
- `src/components/Navbar.tsx` (primary)
- `src/components/UserMenu.tsx` (mobile appearance inside the mobile menu drawer)
- `src/components/NotificationsBell.tsx` ONLY if needed for mobile tap target / positioning

DO NOT TOUCH in stage 1:
- Any profile or admin files
- FavoritesDrawer (stage 2)
- Unrelated home page sections

WHAT TO FIX (ground truth from reading `src/components/Navbar.tsx`):

1. **Mobile navbar header bar (top bar, <= lg breakpoint)**
   - Currently the mobile header shows ONLY the logo on the left and a hamburger on the right. User gets no access to saved favorites, notifications bell, or user avatar without opening the fullscreen menu. This is the #1 production defect — favorites and bell are desktop-only.
   - Add a compact right-side cluster in the mobile header showing (in this order, right-to-left next to hamburger): user avatar/sign-in button, favorites heart (with count badge), notifications bell. These must all render INSIDE the `<lg` viewport.
   - Constraints: stay under a total mobile bar height of 56px. Each icon button 36×36px (tap target expanded via padding to 44×44). Heart + bell use the existing `border border-white/20` white-icon pattern from lines 278, 307, 317 of Navbar.tsx. Avatar: reuse `UserMenu` component — it already handles signed-out/signed-in states. Do NOT recreate the menu, just render it smaller.
   - At 320px width (iPhone SE) with long logo, the cluster must not clip. Hide the avatar name text on mobile (avatar only) — UserMenu already does this because its name is in the dropdown only.
   - The hamburger itself must remain the last (rightmost) item.
   - Hit the `lg:hidden` / `lg:flex` breakpoint exactly — the desktop cluster is currently `hidden lg:flex` (line 215). Add a corresponding `flex lg:hidden` mobile cluster in the header.

2. **Fullscreen mobile menu (when hamburger open)**
   - Current issues:
     a. Menu items use `py-4` + `text-[15px]` which is OK but the border-bottom style looks dated — modernize to row-style items with a subtle chevron icon on the right for single links (not just expandable groups). All expandable groups already have chevrons.
     b. Move `UserMenu` + `NotificationsBell` from the FOOTER of the mobile menu (lines 491-495) to a prominent TOP card at the top of the mobile menu showing: avatar, name, email, and two pill buttons: "My Profile" and "Saved (N)". For signed-out users, show a single "Sign in / Create account" primary card instead. This makes the account state visible the instant the menu opens.
     c. Add a "Saved Properties" row in the mobile menu with the heart icon and the count badge — clicking it opens the FavoritesDrawer (same `window.dispatchEvent(new Event('open-favorites-drawer'))` pattern already in the file at line 124). Close the mobile menu before opening the drawer.
     d. The `pt-32` / hero overlap: scrolled home bg becomes solid at >50px (line 68). On mobile with a short hero, the transparent navbar (when `isHome && !scrolled && !isMobile`) can overlap content. Currently line 50: `setIsMobile(window.innerWidth < 640)` → on mobile `isSolid` is forced true, which is fine — but confirm no regression on desktop.
     e. On iOS Safari, the mobile fullscreen menu must cover the entire viewport including the URL bar area. It currently uses `fixed inset-0 z-[100]` which is correct. Leave it alone.

3. **Currency + Language in mobile menu**
   - The `Globe` icon next to the currency pill (line 450) uses `h-4 w-4 text-accent` — BUT there's no dropdown on mobile; clicking just cycles to the next currency. This is a known workaround but add a `title="Tap to cycle currency"` tooltip for clarity, and make the pill wider so the full currency code is obvious.
   - Language 2-column grid (line 427) is fine; keep it.

4. **Polish**
   - On the navbar PHONE link on mobile (lines 457-465): the blue circle bg `bg-[#3B82F6]/20` with `#60A5FA` icon is off-brand — the rest of the site is green+gold. Change the call icon container to `bg-white/10` with `text-white` icon, and WhatsApp stays as-is (its green is the brand color for WhatsApp).
   - Ensure all mobile tap targets are >= 44×44 (iOS HIG). Audit the X close button on line 349 — already 44×44, good.

DESIGN INTEGRATION CHECK:
- Match existing navbar gradient: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)` (already used).
- Match existing icon button pattern from §2 of PROJECT_CONTEXT.md: `w-9 h-9 rounded-full border border-white/20`. Use this EXACT pattern for the new mobile header heart + bell.
- Floating elements already in page: WhatsApp button (bottom-right) + chat widget. Do NOT add a new fixed floating element.
- Text on the green navbar MUST use `text-white*`, never `text-foreground` (gotcha in PROJECT_CONTEXT.md line 175).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (DEV self-verifies, QA re-verifies live):
1. At 390×844 (iPhone 12), navbar shows logo + heart + bell + avatar + hamburger in one row, no clipping, no horizontal scroll on any page.
2. At 320×568 (iPhone SE), same — if truly too tight, hide bell on <=320px (but favorites heart + avatar must stay).
3. Opening hamburger menu shows: (top) account card with avatar+name+email OR sign-in CTA → (middle) nav links → (language grid) → (currency + contact actions). NotificationsBell + UserMenu are NOT in the footer anymore.
4. Tapping the heart in the mobile navbar opens FavoritesDrawer (same event as desktop).
5. Tapping "Saved Properties" inside the mobile menu closes the menu and opens FavoritesDrawer.
6. Signed-in user avatar on mobile navbar: opens the UserMenu dropdown correctly, dropdown fits on screen (right-edge alignment).
7. Tap targets ≥ 44×44 everywhere in mobile nav.
8. At `lg` (1024px+), desktop nav unchanged — zero regression. Screenshot desktop before/after to confirm.
9. `text-foreground` is never used on the green gradient — grep the final diff.
10. No new `fixed bottom-*` elements added.

DEMO PATH (QA will run these exact steps on mobile viewport 390×844):
1. Open https://staging.binayahhub.com/ → see logo + 3 icon buttons + hamburger in top navbar.
2. Tap heart icon in navbar → FavoritesDrawer slides in from right.
3. Close drawer. Tap hamburger → fullscreen menu slides in.
4. Top of menu shows user card (or sign-in CTA).
5. Scroll menu, expand Insights, tap Communities → navigates to /communities, menu closes.
6. Reopen hamburger, tap "Saved Properties" row → menu closes, drawer opens.
7. Reopen hamburger, tap language EN flag → locale switches, no broken state.
8. At desktop 1440×900: nothing visibly changed vs before.

Testing: run `npm run build` — must pass. Playwright screenshot at 390×844 for `/`, `/search`, `/off-plan` — all navbar should work.
stages.demoPath: 1. Viewport 390x844. 2. Visit /. 3. See heart+bell+avatar+hamburger in top bar. 4. Tap heart -> drawer opens. 5. Tap hamburger -> menu with user card at top. 6. Tap Saved Properties row -> drawer opens. 7. Desktop 1440 -> unchanged.

stages.spec:
STAGE 2 — User profile page redesign + saved properties UX (mobile-first).

PRECONDITION: Stage 1 must be merged. Stage 2 builds on the new mobile nav.

SCOPE (only these files):
- `src/app/[locale]/profile/ProfileClient.tsx` (primary redesign)
- `src/app/[locale]/profile/page.tsx` (layout wrapper; minor adjustments allowed)
- `src/components/FavoritesDrawer.tsx` (cleanup for mobile)
- MAY create: `src/components/SavedPropertiesSection.tsx` — reusable grid component for the profile page (so the profile page shows saved properties inline, not only in the drawer).

DO NOT TOUCH in stage 2:
- Admin pages (stage 3)
- Navbar (stage 1 locked)
- Any API route

WHAT TO FIX (ground truth from reading current files):

Current profile page has:
- Green hero with avatar + name + email
- 3 quick-action cards: Favorites (opens drawer), List a Property, Price Alerts (coming soon)
- "My Submissions" list
- Sign out

Problems:
1. Saved properties are ONLY visible in the drawer; the profile page itself has zero content for saved items — just a button. User expects to SEE their saved properties on their profile.
2. On mobile (< 640px) the `flex items-center gap-6` hero layout crams the 80px avatar + long name in an uncomfortable row.
3. The 3-column quick actions grid becomes a single column on mobile — fine — but cards are oversized (`p-6`) and waste vertical space.
4. Submissions list shows only propertyType + community + price — no thumbnail, no status emphasis, no way to view details. Very utilitarian.
5. No tabs/sections for navigating between Saved, Submissions, Subscriptions. Power users can't find their project subscriptions at all (yet they exist — see `SubscribeButton`).
6. FavoritesDrawer at mobile (currently `max-w-md`) takes ~100% width — good — but its header layout on narrow screens (heart + title + count + clear-all + X) can wrap awkwardly.

WHAT TO BUILD:

A. **Profile hero (mobile + desktop)**
   - Mobile: stack vertically. Center-aligned avatar (72px), name below, email below, 3 stat pills below (Saved N · Submissions M · Subscriptions K). Sign out button top-right of hero (icon-only on mobile, icon+label on desktop).
   - Desktop: current horizontal layout is fine, add the 3 stat pills next to name.
   - Add a subtle member-since line (use `session.user` — but if we don't have createdAt, skip gracefully).

B. **Tabs: Saved · Submissions · Subscriptions**
   - Sticky tab bar below hero, desktop-left-aligned, mobile-horizontal-scroll. Use existing tab button pattern from PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §2: active `bg-[#0B3D2E] text-white`, inactive `bg-background text-muted-foreground`.
   - Query-string driven: `?tab=saved` (default), `?tab=submissions`, `?tab=subscriptions`. Use `useSearchParams` + `router.replace` so back-button works.

C. **Saved tab — NEW `SavedPropertiesSection` component**
   - Reuses the existing `useFavorites()` hook + `/api/favorites/hydrate` pattern from FavoritesDrawer (copy the query logic; don't invent new logic). Factor out if clean.
   - Grid: mobile 1 col, sm 2 cols, lg 3 cols. Card style matches existing property cards on the site (look at `src/app/[locale]/off-plan/` or search results for reference — `rounded-xl`, thumbnail + title + price + community).
   - Each card: image (16:9 thumbnail), title, community, price, "View" link, remove (X) icon button in top-right of card. Clicking card goes to `/property/${slug}` or `/project/${slug}` (same logic as FavoritesDrawer — `p.title ? /property : /project`).
   - Empty state: illustration (heart icon + text), "Browse properties" CTA link to `/search`.
   - Loading state: 3 skeleton cards.
   - Stale detection: FavoritesDrawer currently auto-removes stale ids. Keep that behavior here.

D. **Submissions tab**
   - Keep the existing `/api/list-your-property` fetch.
   - Redesign as a card list (one per submission): property type + community (title), price (subtitle), status badge (right), date (secondary). Mobile: full width card; tablet/desktop: 2-col grid if space.
   - Add a "View details" button that is a no-op with a small "Details coming soon" tooltip (there's no submission detail page yet — don't break scope by creating one).
   - Empty state: CTA to `/list-your-property`.

E. **Subscriptions tab**
   - Use existing `useProjectSubscriptions` hook (mentioned in PROJECT_CONTEXT.md line 258). Verify that path exists: `src/hooks/useProjectSubscriptions.ts`. If the hook does NOT expose a list of subscribed projects (only a boolean/set), skip this tab OR wire it up to fetch `/api/project-subscriptions?mine=1` if that endpoint exists. Grep first; if no endpoint, leave subscriptions as a placeholder "Coming soon" tab (acceptable degradation).
   - If wired: list of subscribed projects with name, developer, unsubscribe button.

F. **FavoritesDrawer mobile polish**
   - Header: stack actions into an overflow menu on <sm (avoid the 5-element row).
   - Make cards 20% taller, bump thumbnail from 80×80 to 96×96 on mobile for readability.
   - Bottom CTA: add a second button "View all in profile" that closes drawer and routes to `/profile?tab=saved`.

G. **Saved-state sync rule (CRITICAL)**
   - When user removes from drawer, the profile Saved tab must update live.
   - When user removes from profile Saved tab, drawer updates live.
   - Both already share `useFavorites()` (which uses localStorage + custom events). Verify no divergent local state in the new SavedPropertiesSection — must read from the hook, never a local copy.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Signed-in user visits /profile → sees hero, 3 tabs, default Saved tab shows their saved properties as a grid.
2. At 390×844: hero is vertical, stat pills wrap nicely, tabs are horizontally scrollable (no overflow).
3. Remove a property from the Saved grid → card disappears immediately, count pill in hero decreases by 1, FavoritesDrawer would reflect the same when opened.
4. Switch to Submissions tab → shows existing submissions with new card design.
5. Switch to Subscriptions tab → either shows subscriptions list OR a graceful "Coming soon".
6. Tab state is URL-driven: share `/profile?tab=submissions` and it opens on that tab.
7. Empty state (user with 0 saved) shows CTA to /search.
8. No new floating elements. No `text-foreground` on green bg.
9. `npm run build` passes.

DEMO PATH:
1. Sign in as test user (dev@prosper-fi.com via Google). 2. Visit /profile at 390×844. 3. See hero with avatar, 3 stat pills, sign-out icon top-right. 4. Below hero: Saved / Submissions / Subscriptions tabs. 5. Saved tab shows grid of saved property cards. 6. Tap X on one → card gone, counter decrements. 7. Switch to Submissions tab → see submission cards. 8. Open FavoritesDrawer from navbar → "View all in profile" CTA navigates to /profile?tab=saved.
stages.demoPath: 1. Sign in. 2. Visit /profile on mobile. 3. See hero + 3 stat pills + sign-out. 4. Default tab = Saved, grid of saved property cards. 5. Remove one -> live update. 6. Tab to Submissions -> redesigned cards. 7. Tab to Subscriptions. 8. FavoritesDrawer has 'View all in profile' button.

stages.spec:
STAGE 3 — Admin dashboard mobile responsiveness + profile menu.

PRECONDITION: Stages 1+2 merged.

SCOPE (only these files):
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/page.tsx` (landing)
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/inquiries/page.tsx`
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/submissions/page.tsx`
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/subscriptions/page.tsx`
- MAY create shared: `src/app/[locale]/admin/AdminHeader.tsx` (client component) — extract the duplicated header bar from the 4 pages above into one component.

DO NOT TOUCH:
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/[token]/page.tsx` (login page — different flow, separate concern)
- Navbar, profile, or non-admin surfaces
- Any API / auth code

WHAT TO FIX (ground truth from reading current admin pages):

1. **Admin header (duplicated across pages) — extract to shared client component**
   - Current: each admin page hand-rolls its `<header className="bg-[#0B3D2E]...">` with breadcrumb + signout. On mobile, the inquiries header has a `flex items-center gap-4` that crowds the breadcrumb and signout together and wraps ugly at 375px.
   - New `AdminHeader.tsx` with props `{ title, backHref?, actions? }`. Renders:
     - Left: hamburger icon (mobile) that opens admin drawer nav / back arrow (when `backHref`)
     - Center: "Binayah Admin" logo + current section title
     - Right: avatar dropdown (re-use `UserMenu` pattern but simpler: avatar → dropdown with "Dashboard", "Sign out")
   - Mobile header: collapse to icon-only back + title + avatar. No loose text like full emails.

2. **Admin landing cards grid**
   - Currently `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-5`. On mobile (1 col) each card is OK, but on a 640-768 tablet the 3-col grid makes cards very narrow with 3 digits awkward.
   - Change to `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3`.
   - Reduce card padding to `p-5` on mobile, `p-6` desktop (`p-5 md:p-6`).
   - Count text: on mobile use `text-2xl`, desktop `text-3xl` (current is always `text-3xl` which can feel oversized on narrow cards).

3. **Admin inquiries / submissions tables — mobile card fallback (CRITICAL)**
   - Currently `overflow-x-auto` on the table. On mobile, admin has to horizontal-scroll a 6-column table. Unusable in practice.
   - Solution: duplicate-render the data. Desktop (`hidden md:block`) = current table. Mobile (`md:hidden`) = vertical card list.
   - Mobile card for inquiry: name + contact (email/phone stacked) + source pill + type pill + message (line-clamp-3) + date at bottom. Clicking/tapping expands to show full message (state: `expandedId`).
   - Mobile card for submission: same structure with propertyType + community + asking price + status badge + date.
   - Same data source — just two rendering modes behind a Tailwind breakpoint.

4. **Admin subscriptions page**
   - Read `src/app/[locale]/admin/subscriptions/page.tsx` first. Likely also a wide table. Apply the same mobile card fallback.

5. **Admin "profile" / avatar menu**
   - User asked for "profile admin" — interpret as: the admin should have a visible identity widget showing who's logged in, with a dropdown to sign out and optionally go back to the main site.
   - Current admin landing shows avatar + name + email inline — keep the spirit but move to a dropdown on mobile so the email doesn't force awkward wrapping.
   - Create `AdminUserMenu` (or inline in `AdminHeader`): avatar button → popover with name, email, "View main site" link to `/`, "Sign out" button.

6. **Styling consistency with main site**
   - Current admin uses `bg-gray-50` page, `bg-white` cards. Acceptable but slightly different from the main site which uses `bg-background` + `bg-card`. Since admin is a separate surface, it's OK to keep gray-50. Do NOT refactor the whole color system.
   - But: admin's `#0B3D2E` header must match the main navbar hex — confirmed it does.

7. **No regressions on desktop**
   - Every admin view at 1440×900 must look substantially the same as before (the current desktop design shipped in commit `1565fe9a` just 1 iteration ago and was approved). Mobile cards are the only NEW visual — do not redesign desktop tables.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Admin routes (`/en/admin`, `/en/admin/inquiries`, `/en/admin/submissions`, `/en/admin/subscriptions`) all render without horizontal scroll at 390×844.
2. `AdminHeader` extracted; all 4 pages use it; ~50-80 lines of duplication gone (measurable in diff).
3. Mobile: inquiries + submissions + subscriptions show card lists, not tables. Desktop: tables unchanged.
4. Avatar dropdown in admin header works on mobile and desktop; "Sign out" logs out and returns to `/en/admin` (current behavior in line 106 of admin page.tsx).
5. Admin landing at tablet 768px shows 2-col card grid not 3-col cramped.
6. `npm run build` passes; TypeScript strict — no `any` on new props.
7. QA auth seed (`/api/test/auth-seed`) still lets QA into admin — don't alter auth logic.

DEMO PATH:
1. Auth as admin (Google OAuth with allowlisted email). 2. Visit /en/admin at 390×844 → dashboard cards in single column, clean header. 3. Tap avatar top-right → dropdown with name, email, View main site, Sign out. 4. Tap Inquiries card → /en/admin/inquiries shows mobile card list (not table). 5. Expand one card → full message shown. 6. Desktop 1440×900: tables + grid unchanged from stage 0 design.
stages.demoPath: 1. Admin auth. 2. /en/admin at 390x844 -> single column cards, clean header, avatar dropdown. 3. /en/admin/inquiries -> card list (not horizontal scroll table). 4. Tap card -> expands to show full message. 5. Desktop 1440 -> tables and grid look like before.

plan:
STAGE 3 — Admin dashboard mobile responsiveness + profile menu.

PRECONDITION: Stages 1+2 merged.

SCOPE (only these files):
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/page.tsx` (landing)
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/inquiries/page.tsx`
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/submissions/page.tsx`
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/subscriptions/page.tsx`
- MAY create shared: `src/app/[locale]/admin/AdminHeader.tsx` (client component) — extract the duplicated header bar from the 4 pages above into one component.

DO NOT TOUCH:
- `src/app/[locale]/admin/[token]/page.tsx` (login page — different flow, separate concern)
- Navbar, profile, or non-admin surfaces
- Any API / auth code

WHAT TO FIX (ground truth from reading current admin pages):

1. **Admin header (duplicated across pages) — extract to shared client component**
   - Current: each admin page hand-rolls its `<header className="bg-[#0B3D2E]...">` with breadcrumb + signout. On mobile, the inquiries header has a `flex items-center gap-4` that crowds the breadcrumb and signout together and wraps ugly at 375px.

     - Center: "Binayah Admin" logo + current section title

   - Mobile header: collapse to icon-only back + title + avatar. No loose text like full emails.

2. **Admin landing cards grid**
   - Currently `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3 gap-5`. On mobile (1 col) each card is OK, but on a 640-768 tablet the 3-col grid makes cards very narrow with 3 digits awkward.
   - Change to `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3`.

4. **Admin subscriptions page**

5. **Admin "profile" / avatar menu**
   - User asked for "profile admin" — interpret as: the admin should have a visible identity widget showing who's logged in, with a dropdown to sign out and optionally go back to the main site.

6. **Styling consistency with main site**

7. **No regressions on desktop**
   - Every admin view at 1440×900 must look substantially the same as before (the current desktop design shipped in commit `1565fe9a` just 1 iteration ago and was approved). Mobile cards are the only NEW visual — do not redesign desktop tables.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. Admin routes (`/en/admin`, `/en/admin/inquiries`, `/en/admin/submissions`, `/en/admin/subscriptions`) all render without horizontal scroll at 390×844.
2. `AdminHeader` extracted; all 4 pages use it; ~50-80 lines of duplication gone (measurable in diff).
3. Mobile: inquiries + submissions + subscriptions show card lists, not tables. Desktop: tables unchanged.
4. Avatar dropdown in admin header works on mobile and desktop; "Sign out" logs out and returns to `/en/admin` (current behavior in line 106 of admin page.tsx).
5. Admin landing at tablet 768px shows 2-col card grid not 3-col cramped.
6. `npm run build` passes; TypeScript strict — no `any` on new props.
7. QA auth seed (`/api/test/auth-seed`) still lets QA into admin — don't alter auth logic.

DEMO PATH:
1. Auth as admin (Google OAuth with allowlisted email). 2. Visit /en/admin at 390×844 → dashboard cards in single column, clean header. 3. Tap avatar top-right → dropdown with name, email, View main site, Sign out. 4. Tap Inquiries card → /en/admin/inquiries shows mobile card list (not table). 5. Expand one card → full message shown. 6. Desktop 1440×900: tables + grid unchanged from stage 0 design.

devReport.summary: Admin dashboard: AdminHeader client component extracted (avatar dropdown w/ name/email/Dashboard/View site/Sign out); 4 admin pages updated to use it; landing grid fixed to 1→2→3 col; mobile card fallbacks added to inquiries/submissions/subscriptions pages (md:hidden cards + hidden md:block tables); no auth/API changes

findings.issue: Hardcoded /en/admin in Dashboard link + sign-out callbackUrl — pre-existing pattern, non-blocking

tested: Stage 1 — Mobile navbar at 390x844: bell, heart, avatar, hamburger in top bar
Stage 1 — Hamburger opens fullscreen menu with Saved Properties row
Stage 2 — FavoritesDrawer opens via heart button (single drawer, no double)
Stage 2 — FavoritesDrawer has View all in Profile button (code-verified; requires saved items)
Stage 2 — Profile page source confirms 3-tab bar (Saved/Submissions/Subscriptions)
Stage 3 — Admin page source confirms grid-cols-1 (mobile) / sm:grid-cols-2 / lg:grid-cols-3
Stage 3 — Admin inquiries source confirms md:hidden card list + hidden md:block desktop table
Stage 3 — No horizontal scroll on admin pages
Cross-stage regression — Desktop nav unchanged (Buy, Rent, Get in Touch all present)
Cross-stage regression — /en/buy and /en/off-plan load without errors
Cross-stage regression — No console errors on desktop or mobile home page

bugs.description: Atlas DNS unreachable — NextAuth CLIENT_FETCH_ERROR causes auth-seed session not to persist; /en/profile redirects to /signin
bugs.reproSteps: 1. GET /api/test/auth-seed (returns 200 ok:true). 2. GET /api/auth/session → returns {}. 3. Navigate to /en/profile → redirects to /signin.
bugs.expected: Auth session persists, profile page renders with tab bar
bugs.actual: NextAuth MongoDB adapter cannot reach Atlas cluster0.bxh2ywj.mongodb.net; session is empty; profile redirects to signin

bugs.description: Admin page shows Unauthorized view — isAdminSession() hits MongoDB (Atlas DNS unreachable) and fails; admin dashboard stat cards cannot be tested at runtime
bugs.reproSteps: 1. Navigate to /en/admin. 2. Page renders 🔒 Unauthorized or MongoDB error.
bugs.expected: Admin dashboard with stat cards and responsive grid
bugs.actual: Unauthorized view due to Atlas DNS ENOTFOUND in sandbox. Source code confirms correct responsive grid (grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3).

bugs.description: Pre-existing Unsplash 404 in WhatWeOffer.tsx, not introduced by this diff

summary: SHIP. All 3 stages passed local QA + regression sweep, QA-LIVE passed on https://staging.binayahhub.com, and post-QA-LIVE UX fix (profile hero pill buttons replaced with stat chips, SHA 06c0358b) is deployed. Pre-existing Unsplash 404 in WhatWeOffer.tsx is out of scope for this task.

stageHistory.summary: Mobile navbar cluster (bell+heart+avatar+hamburger) + fullscreen menu account card + Saved Properties row
Profile page redesign (hero + tabs + saved grid + submissions + subscriptions) + FavoritesDrawer mobile polish + double-mount fix
Admin mobile: AdminHeader shared component + mobile card lists for inquiries/submissions/subscriptions + 2-col tablet grid
